title: Grilled Smoky Turkish Lamb Kebap
description: A flavorful and aromatic Turkish kebap made with tender lamb, crispy bread, and crumbly cheese. Serve immediately to experience the full smoky flavor.
instructions: Mix lamb, Grana Padano cheese, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper in a large bowl until just combined.
Do not overmix the meat mixture, as it will become tough.
Form the mixture into 8-10 skewers, about 3-4 inches each.
Thread a slice of bread onto the skewer, followed by a piece of lamb, a few slices of red onion, and a sprinkle of chopped garlic.
Repeat the process until the skewer is filled, leaving a small space at the end for easy handling.
Brush the grill grates with a small amount of oil to prevent sticking.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the lamb reaches an internal temperature of 63C.
While the skewers are cooking, toast the bread slices on the grill until crispy.
Once the skewers are cooked, brush them with a little oil and wrap each one in a large Ti leaf.
Arrange on a plate and serve immediately with the toasted bread on the side.
